questions.question: Which of the following is a non-renewable natural resources?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable resource because it cannot be replenished naturally within a short period.

questions.question: The demand for a factor input as a result of the demand for its output is known as
questions.explanation: Derived demand occurs when the demand for a factor is based on the demand for the final product.

questions.question: If the government stops subsidy on cocoa production, the supply curve of cocoa will
questions.explanation: Removing subsidy increases production costs, shifting the supply curve to the left.

questions.question: A monopolist may enjoy abnormal profit only if its
questions.explanation: Abnormal profit occurs when price exceeds average total cost.

questions.question: The supply of light could be met using a candle, lantern and gas lamp. This is an example of a
questions.explanation: Different sources producing the same good are an example of joint supply.

questions.question: Industries are sometimes located in areas which are not profitable so as to

options.C: bring about external economies of scale
options.D: be able to reap internal economies of scale

questions.explanation: Locating industries in less profitable areas promotes balanced regional development.

questions.question: A government may cause a favourable change in her invisible trade account by

options.C: encouraging people to go for medical treatment abroad
options.D: increasing the export of her primary products

questions.explanation: Developing services like tourism boosts invisible exports.

questions.question: To overcome the problem of double counting under the expenditure approach,

options.A: spending on intermediate goods and inputs are considered
options.C: proper accounting standards must be used
options.D: only spending on final goods are considered

questions.explanation: Counting only final goods prevents double counting.

questions.question: At what point on the total product curve shown above will marginal product be negative?
questions.explanation: Marginal product becomes negative after the point where total product starts to decline.

questions.question: National income is calculated mainly to

options.A: determine how income is to shared
options.C: determine aid to be received from other nations
options.D: determine the level of sectoral performance

questions.explanation: National income measures the overall economic performance.

questions.question: Assisting members to solve temporary balance of payments problems is the reason for the establishment of the
questions.explanation: IMF provides financial assistance for balance of payments issues.

questions.question: Money is able to function more as a medium of exchange when

options.A: there is a fall in production
options.B: more people engage in subsistence production

questions.explanation: Specialization increases the need for money as a medium of exchange.

questions.question: An economy system is mainly concerned with

options.A: studying the relationship between ends and means
options.B: the allocation of scarce resources between alternative ends

questions.explanation: Economics deals with resource allocation.

questions.question: When the marginal utility from the consumption of commodity X (Mux) is greater than the price of commodity X (Px), a rational consumer should

options.B: consume the same quantity of commodity X

questions.explanation: Consume more until marginal utility equals price.

questions.question: Which of the following items is not classified as working capital?
questions.explanation: Equipment is a fixed asset, not working capital.

questions.question: If the demand for a commodity remains constant as price increases, the commodity is said to be
questions.explanation: Price inelastic demand means quantity demanded does not change with price.

questions.question: Warehousing is an economic activity that falls under
questions.explanation: Warehousing is part of the tertiary sector.

questions.question: The study of economics is mainly concerned with how to

options.A: rank individuals' wants given the abundant resources
options.B: make choice when resources are inadequate
options.C: satisfy every member of all societies
options.D: produce all the goods needed by everyone

questions.explanation: Economics is about making choices with limited resources.

questions.question: Use the graph above to answer the question. The curve labelled W shows the
questions.explanation: A progressive tax increases with income or wealth.

questions.question: Resources are pooled for the mutual benefit of its members mainly in a
questions.explanation: Cooperatives pool resources for mutual benefit.

questions.question: The total fixed cost curve is
questions.explanation: Fixed costs remain constant regardless of output, forming a horizontal curve.

questions.question: A small scale firm located within an industrial estate will enjoy
questions.explanation: External economies come from the environment or location, like industrial estates.

questions.question: Country M enjoys absolute advantage over country N in the production of commodities X and Y, if country M

options.A: is inefficient in the production of both commodities X and Y
options.B: is more efficient than country N in producing both commodities X and Y
options.C: can produce only commodity Y cheaper than country N
options.D: and country N are efficient in the production of both goods

questions.explanation: Absolute advantage means more efficient production in both goods.

questions.question: Commercial banks help in economic development mainly by

options.A: acting as trustees and executors of wills
options.B: acting as intermediaries between government and borrowers
options.D: giving financial advice to the government

questions.explanation: Banks facilitate investments and economic activities.

questions.question: When job vacancies are publicized, the government is mainly trying to solve the problem of
questions.explanation: Frictional unemployment relates to matching jobs and workers.

questions.question: An indication that there is inflation in a country is that

options.A: there is a decrease in the demand for goods and services
options.B: there are too many goods in circulation
options.C: the same amount of money buys lower quantity of goods
options.D: people prefer to lend than to borrow

questions.explanation: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money.

questions.question: An example of government's recurrent expenditure is

options.B: the cost of building a school

questions.explanation: Salaries are recurring expenses.

questions.question: A fall in the price of a normal commodity which has elastic demand will result in
questions.explanation: For elastic demand, lowering price increases total revenue.

questions.question: The dependency ratio of a country is the

options.A: the children and aged who rely on the active population for support
options.B: people who are cared for by their extended families
options.C: total active population who depend on government for survival
options.D: number of children who depend on their parents for survival

questions.explanation: Dependency ratio measures non-working dependents.

questions.question: When the demand for a commodity increases while supply remains unchanged, the equilibrium price and quantity will
questions.explanation: Demand increase pushes price and quantity up.

questions.question: Consumers have access to a variety of goods through the activities of the
questions.explanation: Retailers directly serve consumers.

questions.question: The Malthusian theory of population is best illustrated when

options.A: the size of the population and available resources are equal
options.B: both population and food supply increase at the same rate
options.C: food supply increases much faster than population growth
options.D: population increases much faster than food supply

questions.explanation: Malthus predicted population growth exceeds food supply.

questions.question: The Net National Product (NNP) of a country is $50m while the depreciation allowance is $10m. The Gross National Product (GNP) is
questions.explanation: GNP = NNP + depreciation = 50 + 10 = 60 million.

questions.question: The rate of growth of population is

options.A: initial population plus number of births and net migration
options.B: the difference between birth rate and death rate
options.C: birth rate less death rate plus net migration
options.D: the number of immigrants plus number of births

questions.explanation: Population growth = (birth rate - death rate) + net migration.

questions.question: The price level and the value of money are
questions.explanation: As price levels rise, money's value decreases.

questions.question: One of the factors that may not promote industrial development is

options.A: local firms entering into partnerships with foreign firms
options.B: granting capital to firms at reasonable interest rates
options.C: setting up industrial estates with modern amenities

questions.explanation: Old firms' tax exemptions do not promote new industrial growth.

questions.question: If a firm retrenches some of its factory workers its

options.D: fixed cost is likely to increase

questions.explanation: Retrenchment reduces variable costs.

questions.question: Many workers are employed in the agricultural sector of developing countries because

options.A: labour intensive method is mostly adopted
options.C: wages in the sector is high
options.D: they practice mechanized system of farming

questions.explanation: Labour-intensive methods require more workers.

questions.question: Taxes levied on goods and services by government are called
questions.explanation: Taxes on goods/services are indirect taxes.

questions.question: The average revenue curve of a firm in a perfect market is the same as the

options.B: average cost curve of the firm
options.D: total revenue curve of the firm

questions.explanation: In perfect competition, AR equals the demand curve faced by the firm.

questions.question: The most liquid asset among the following is
questions.explanation: Cash is the most liquid asset.

questions.question: Petrol and Kerosene are jointly obtained from crude oil. If the supply of petrol increases, the

options.A: cost of crude oil production has increased
options.C: supply of kerosene will remain unchanged

questions.explanation: An increase in petrol supply can lead to increased kerosene supply if they are joint products.

questions.question: The fixing of maximum prices by government is mainly on
questions.explanation: Price ceilings are often imposed on essential goods.

questions.question: When more of tax on a product is borne by the buyer than the seller, the commodity involved has
questions.explanation: Tax burden falls more on inelastic demand.

questions.question: The marks obtained by 9 students in a class test are recorded below: 9, 8, 7, 5,7, 6, 8, 9 and 7. What is the median mark?
questions.explanation: Median is the middle value when data is ordered.

questions.question: Tariffs, quotas and embargoes are examples of
questions.explanation: Trade restrictions are policies to limit imports/exports.

questions.question: Trade protection will assist economic growth in developing countries if it can

options.A: allow the importation of cheaper goods people want
options.D: generate enough revenue through export duties

questions.explanation: Protection helps new industries grow.

questions.question: A country is described as developing if

options.A: the income per head is low
options.B: the contribution of tertiary sector to national income is high

questions.explanation: Low income per capita indicates a developing country.

questions.question: A joint stock company enjoys perpetual existence because

options.A: there is a higher degree of specialization
options.C: it is formed by a minimum of seven members

questions.explanation: Legal incorporation gives perpetual existence.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a problem facing Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS)?
questions.explanation: Inadequate labor supply is not typically a problem for ECOWAS.

images.alt: Graph showing total product curve with points P, Q, R, S
